title: Ouratea pinetorum
taxonomy: Ouratea pinetorum is classified within the family Ochnaceae, which is a family of flowering plants. This family, along with the related family Strasburgeriaceae, is part of the order Malpighiales. The order Malpighiales is a large group within the clade rosids, which is one of the two main groups of flowering plants. Ouratea pinetorum is a species within the genus Ouratea, which consists of several species found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Ouratea pinetorum reflects its evolutionary relationships and phylogenetic position among other flowering plants.

Its leaves are said to have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, making it a potential remedy for skin infections and wounds. Ouratea pinetorum is also used to treat fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal problems. intro: Ouratea pinetorum is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae, commonly known as the Ouratea or ouratea vine. Ouratea pinetorum has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Ouratea pinetorum is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used as a topical ointment or cream to treat skin conditions and wounds.
